aesthetic: The Inventory of Effects — Fiore's collage grotesque
anchor: Quentin Fiore and Jerome Agel's 1967 paperback design of The Medium is the Massage (and War and Peace in the Global Village, 1968): full-bleed black-and-white photo-collage, oversized Akzidenz/Helvetica grotesque bleeding across the gutter, big numerals and the mosaic page — crossed with McLuhan's own hot/cool axis (a hot signal-red against a cool cyan) and the teletype grid of 'typographic man'.
rationale: The corpus's primary artifacts ARE designed typographic objects — the Massage and War and Peace are collage books that enact their own thesis in form, so the aesthetic is the evidence, not a costume. The grotesque display is Fiore's signature; the serif body is the discursive, literary McLuhan of the trivium and Joyce; the teletype mono is 'typographic man'. The five hues are read straight off McLuhan's categories: hot media (red), cool media (cyan), the electric/acoustic (magenta), the printed/visual (ink graphite), and the warm oral (amber). Sharp-cornered, high-contrast, oracular — a page that massages the eye.

fonts.display: 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, 'Liberation Sans', system-ui, sans-serif
fonts.body: Georgia, 'Iowan Old Style', Palatino, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', Consolas, 'DejaVu Sans Mono', ui-monospace, monospace
